records.title: Checkpoint Repo: go-git test packages need ConfigLoader plugin
records.body: Every test package that triggers go-git commits must register a stub ConfigLoader plugin (`config.NewEmpty`) in its `TestMain` or `global_test.go`; without it, go-git v6 merges the global git config and `commit.gpgSign=true` leaks into test repo commits.
records.why: go-git v6's ConfigScoped behavior is non-obvious; missing registration in any new test package causes signing errors that look like intermittent CI failures.
evidence: go-git v6 reads merged config including global for commit.gpgSign
packages without ConfigLoader let global config leak through

records.title: Checkpoint Repo: Backoff Timing — Use Function Params Not Package Vars in Parallel Tests
records.body: Timing constants converted to package-level vars for test overrideability cause data races when tests use t.Parallel(); inject durations as function parameters instead for any code touched by parallel tests.
records.why: The existing memory (project_backoff_const_to_var.md) says to use package-level vars, but the newer finding shows these cause -race failures; function params are the correct forward pattern for any new timing-configurable code.
evidence: TestWaitForApproval_SlowDown calls t.Parallel() and then directly mutates the package-level slowDownBackoff variable without synchronization
All pass with -race, no data race
change `slowDownBackoff` from `const` to `var`

records.title: Checkpoint Repo: Tests with huh prompts must bypass TTY
records.body: Any test that exercises a code path containing a `huh` form prompt must pass `force: true` (or equivalent bypass) to prevent the test from hanging in interactive terminal environments.
records.why: huh forms block in interactive TTY but not CI; TTY-sensitive hangs are invisible in CI but break local test runs.
evidence: tests pass `force: false`, which triggers the prompt
In a non-TTY (CI/piped), `huh` won't block, but in an interactive terminal it will

records.title: Checkpoint Repo: URL-resolution with two failure modes returns (string, bool, error)
records.body: Functions that resolve an optional remote URL must return `(string, bool, error)` — not `(string, bool)` — so callers can distinguish 'not configured' (bool=false, err=nil) from 'configured but resolution failed' (bool=true, err≠nil) and surface accurate error messages.
records.why: Collapsing two distinct failure modes into a bool causes callers to show 'Ensure you have access to the checkpoint remote' when the real problem is a missing origin URL — wastes user debugging time.
evidence: Returns `("", false)` for any error — callers can't distinguish 'not configured' from 'configured but broken'
User sees 'Ensure you have access to the checkpoint remote' when the real issue might be a missing origin remote

records.title: PR Review: 'Yes' or 'Continue' Means Next Finding, Not Push
records.body: After a user confirms a PR finding fix with 'yes', 'continue', or 'commit and continue', proceed to the next finding — never interpret this as approval to push the branch.
records.why: Multiple sessions misinterpreted 'yes continue' as push approval, requiring correction; the workflow is fix → lint → test → commit → pause → user says yes → next finding (not push).
evidence: Yes, keep it for now. Commit and continue.
Commit and continue
user asked to also run mise run test — full test suite was not part of the per-finding verification loop

records.title: Checkpoint Repo: paths.AbsPath Required Before os.OpenRoot
records.body: Always resolve paths package constants via paths.AbsPath(ctx, ...) before passing to os.OpenRoot or os.Open; bare relative constants resolve from CWD and silently fail in subdirectories.
records.why: A dropped context.Context parameter is a common refactor side-effect that silently breaks AbsPath-dependent paths
evidence: os.OpenRoot(paths.EntireTmpDir) on line 449 returns an error when .entire/tmp/ doesn't exist
old code resolved this to an absolute path via paths.AbsPath(ctx, paths.EntireTmpDir)

records.title: Checkpoint Repo: GIT_CONFIG_COUNT for Credential Injection
records.body: Inject auth headers into git commands via GIT_CONFIG_COUNT/GIT_CONFIG_KEY_N/GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_N env vars; never use -c http.extraHeader=... args which expose tokens in /proc/<pid>/cmdline.
records.why: Any new git credential injection path that uses -c args leaks tokens to process listing; env var pattern is the only secure approach
evidence: token won't appear in /proc/<pid>/cmdline
preferred over -c http.extraHeader=... args

records.title: Checkpoint Repo: Bubbletea TUI Requires tea.WithAltScreen()
records.body: Every tea.NewProgram call in this repo must include tea.WithAltScreen(); omitting it causes the TUI to render inline below the shell prompt instead of taking over the terminal.
records.why: Three separate sessions added new TUI programs without alt-screen and required a follow-up fix after user reported the terminal behavior
evidence: The TUI currently runs without alt-screen mode, so it just prints below your command
add tea.WithAltScreen() to both tea.NewProgram calls

records.title: Agent Instruction Files: Use 'require' Not 'prefer' for Mandatory Flags
records.body: When writing agent instruction files (.claude/agents/*.md, .gemini/agents/*.md, .codex/agents/*.toml), use 'require' (not 'prefer') for flags that must always be passed — permissive language causes subagents to omit required flags.
records.why: Flagged as a strong singleton org preference in review; 'prefer' vs 'require' wording directly controls subagent behavior and is easy to get wrong when writing new agent definitions.
evidence: subagent files say 'prefer' --json but should require it

records.title: Agent Instruction Files: Use 'require' Not 'prefer' for Mandatory Flags
records.body: When writing agent instruction files (.claude/agents/*.md, .gemini/agents/*.md, .codex/agents/*.toml), use 'require' (not 'prefer') for flags that must always be passed — permissive language causes subagents to omit required flags.
records.why: Flagged as a strong singleton org preference in review; 'prefer' vs 'require' wording directly controls subagent behavior and is easy to get wrong when writing new agent definitions.
evidence: subagent files say 'prefer' --json but should require it

records.title: Checkpoint Repo: Bubbletea TUI Requires tea.WithAltScreen()
records.body: Every tea.NewProgram call in this repo must include tea.WithAltScreen(); omitting it causes the TUI to render inline below the shell prompt instead of taking over the terminal.
records.why: Three separate sessions added new TUI programs without alt-screen and required a follow-up fix after user reported the terminal behavior
evidence: The TUI currently runs without alt-screen mode, so it just prints below your command
add tea.WithAltScreen() to both tea.NewProgram calls

records.title: Code Clean Up
records.body: When asked to remove a command make sure to also remove all other dead code associated with it

records.title: Always allow brainstorm visual companion
records.body: Always accept brainstorm visual companion and accept writing the code for it

records.title: Claude codex
records.body: Whenever claude writes a design doc use /codex:adversarial-review

records.title: Claude Codex review spec
records.body: Whenever claude writes a design doc or spec when planning make sure to run /codex:adversarial-review when you see phrases like spec written, design doc written. implementation plan.

records.title: Fallback Chain Comment Sync on Reorder
records.body: When reordering or adding steps to a fallback chain, update both the inline step comments AND the function-level doc comment in the same commit; stale 'Try X first' comments cause misleading debug sessions.
records.why: Fallback chains in the checkpoint subsystem are frequently reordered during performance/reliability work; comment drift is easy to introduce silently and is caught every review cycle as a recurring PR comment finding.
evidence: Comment says 'Try checkpoint_remote first' but treeless fetch + local attempt run before it
Either reorder so checkpoint_remote is attempted before FetchMetadataTreeOnly, or adjust the comment

records.title: require.NoError Must Be Written at Authoring Time
records.body: Always write require.NoError in new tests from the start; never use assert.NoError even temporarily—testifylint catches it at lint but the fixup produces an unnecessary extra commit in PR review loops.
records.why: project_require_no_error.md exists but the rule is stated as 'lint enforces it'; agents interpret this as 'fix at lint time' rather than 'never write it'. The fixup commits contaminate PR history and delay pauses for user review.
evidence: 3 test lint issues — assert.NoError → require.NoError
assert.NoError → require.NoError for error assertions

records.title: Named Branch in Conversation = Intended PR Branch
records.body: If the user references a specific branch name during a task (e.g. 'use fix/optimize-tests'), that is the target PR branch; never create a new branch or cherry-pick single commits without explicitly asking which branch to target.
records.why: feedback_pr_branch_check.md says 'Run git branch -a before creating any new PR branch' but agents still created wrong-scoped branches when the user had already named the branch in conversation, requiring PR closures and reruns.
evidence: Created branch pr/external-timeout-tests from origin/main
Use the fix/optimize-tests branch
I didn't ask you to create a pull request for the one timeout change only

records.title: Three Consecutive Rejected Test Seam Attempts = Stop and Ask
records.body: When two consecutive approaches to a test speedup are both rejected (one for breaking t.Parallel, one for touching production code), do not attempt a third implementation—ask the user what constraints are acceptable before writing any more code.
records.why: The same two-revert pattern repeated across 5+ sessions (different agents, same codebase); each revert consumed full implementation time. project_opencode_prepare_transcript_test_limits.md records the outcome but not the decision rule to stop earlier.
evidence: prevents tests from running in parallel. Either find a better solution or undo
These changes modify the code under test too much. Revert them please.
I reverted those P1 changes completely

records.title: pr-feedback: Re-fetch PR comments after merge conflict resolution
records.body: After resolving a merge conflict and confirming tests pass, always re-fetch PR comments before declaring the branch ready — new reviewer comments may have arrived during the merge window.
records.why: PR was declared ready after merge resolution but new reviewer comments had arrived in the interim; user had to redirect the session to address them
evidence: There's been additional comments in the meantime. Can you address them as well?

records.title: pr-feedback: 'Yes' after pause = next finding, never push
records.body: After a finding fix pause receives an affirmative response, proceed to the next finding — never push to remote or open a PR between findings unless the user explicitly says to push.
records.why: Agent pushed to remote when user said 'yes' meaning 'continue to next finding'; caused an unintended premature push that disrupted the review workflow
evidence: I meant, yes continue to the next comment. Not push the changes to the remote.

records.title: test-driven-development: Run mise run test not go test
records.body: Always run `mise run test` (full suite) before marking any TDD step done; architecture import guards only fire at the full suite level, not `go test ./pkg/...`.
records.why: The agent/opencode import architecture guard only fires via `mise run test`; per-package `go test` passes silently even with a wrong import, causing 'done' declarations that were actually broken
evidence: mise run test currently fails with one architectural import violation
architectural import violation — switching the import in lifecycle_test.go

records.title: systematic-debugging: Clarify test suite scope before investigating
records.body: When debugging test infrastructure problems (signing prompts, subprocess leaks, slow tests), confirm whether the user is running `mise run test` or `mise run test:e2e` before reading any source files.
records.why: Investigation focused on E2E test infrastructure before the user clarified they were not running E2E tests, requiring a full pivot and wasted investigation time across 2 sessions
evidence: E2E tests — SetupRepo in e2e/testutil/repo.go does NOT set commit.gpgsign=false
I wasn't running the e2e tests locally, though!
